title: AMC Entertainment Holdings Inc Reports Second Quarter 2024 Results
text: AMC reported challenging second quarter results, with total revenues declining to $1,030.6 million from $1,347.9 million year-over-year. The company posted a net loss of $32.8 million compared to earnings of $8.6 million in Q2 2023. Adjusted EBITDA fell significantly to $29.4 million from $182.5 million. CEO Adam Aron noted that the quarter started slowly due to lingering effects from 2023 industry strikes but gained momentum late in the period. Inside Out 2 proved transformative, and the company achieved its highest-ever June Adjusted EBITDA in its company's entire 104-year history, according to Aron's statement. Subsequent months showed continued strength, with Deadpool & Wolverine driving record attendance for an R-rated film opening weekend. AMC completed significant debt restructuring during Q3 2024, extending approximately $2.45 billion of debt previously due in 2026 to 2029 and beyond. The company raised $250 million through stock sales and exchanged debt for equity during the quarter. The company maintained $770.3 million in cash as of June 30, 2024, positioning itself to benefit from anticipated blockbuster releases through 2026.
